summaryText: Guest Speaker Scott Williams opened by acknowledging the unique tensions and relational challenges that often surface during the holiday season, using humorous personal travel stories to illustrate how people and situations can 'drive us crazy.' He connected this common frustration to a deeper spiritual truth: often, the person who frustrates us most is the one in the mirror, and we are weighed down by our own unresolved 'baggage.' The central message was drawn from 1 Samuel 10, where Saul, chosen to be king, was found hiding among the baggage. Pastor Scott used this as a powerful metaphor for the emotional and spiritual burdens we carry that prevent us from stepping into God's calling. He presented a practical, three-step acronym—D.I.G.—to help believers 'check their baggage': Determine what you're carrying, Inventory what you need to let go of, and Give it to God. The sermon emphasized that we all carry something, whether it's regret, hurt, worry, or grief, and that these burdens have a cost. He encouraged the congregation to stop carrying other people's baggage and to stop using the 'broken bricks' of their past to build their future. The closing call was to surrender these burdens to the Lord, trusting that He cares for the righteous and will not let them fall, culminating in an invitation for salvation for those ready to begin a new life with Christ.
mainPoints: We must Determine the specific emotional and spiritual baggage we are carrying, as we all carry something that hinders us.
We need to take Inventory of our burdens and consciously decide what we need to let go of, such as past hurts, regrets, or worries.
The final and most critical step is to Give our burdens to God, trusting Him to care for us and prevent us from falling, which brings true freedom.

versesMentioned.text: But though I have rescued you from your misery and distress, you have rejected your God today and have said, ‘No, we want a king instead!’ Now therefore present yourselves before the LORD by your tribes and clans.” ... So they asked the LORD, “Where is he?” And the LORD replied, “He is hiding among the baggage.”
Give your burdens to the LORD, and he will take care of you. He will not permit the godly to slip and fall.
“I am sorry that I ever made Saul king, for he has not been loyal to me and has refused to obey my command.”

Monday.scripture: Psalm 55:22 (NIV) - Cast your cares on the LORD and he will sustain you; he will never let the righteous be shaken.
Monday.action: Take 5 minutes of quiet reflection to honestly name one specific piece of 'baggage' you've been carrying (e.g., a regret, a worry, a past hurt). Write it down.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to be honest with myself and with You about what I am carrying. Give me the courage to name it.

Tuesday.scripture: Matthew 11:28-29 (NIV) - Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls.
Tuesday.action: Look at the burden you named yesterday. Ask yourself: 'What is the cost of carrying this? Am I ready to let it go?' Make a conscious decision to release it.
Tuesday.prayer: God, I inventory this burden and choose to release my grip on it. I acknowledge that holding onto it is too heavy for me.

Wednesday.scripture: 1 Peter 5:7 (NIV) - Cast all your anxiety on him because he cares for you.
Wednesday.action: Find a quiet space. Verbally or in writing, present the burden you've identified to God. Use the simple prayer: 'God, I give [name the burden] to You. I trust You with it.'
Wednesday.prayer: Heavenly Father, I am giving [name the burden] to You right now. I trust that You care for me and will handle what I cannot.

Thursday.scripture: Philippians 4:6-7 (NIV) - Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God. And the peace of God, which transcends all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus.
Thursday.focus: Resisting the Urge to Take It Back
Thursday.action: When worry or old thoughts about your burden arise today, pause and say, 'I have given that to God.' Redirect your thoughts to a promise from Scripture.
Thursday.prayer: Lord, when I am tempted to reclaim my burden, remind me that I have entrusted it to You. Fill me with Your peace that guards my heart.

Friday.scripture: Galatians 6:2 (NIV) - Carry each other’s burdens, and in this way you will fulfill the law of Christ.
Friday.action: Be mindful not to take on someone else's 'baggage' today. If a conversation turns to dumping heavy problems, lovingly offer to pray with them or point them to church resources instead of carrying it yourself.
Friday.prayer: God, give me wisdom and love to support others without taking on burdens that belong to You or to them. Help me point people to You.

Saturday.scripture: Isaiah 40:31 (NIV) - but those who hope in the LORD will renew their strength. They will soar on wings like eagles; they will run and not grow weary, they will walk and not be faint.
Saturday.action: Do something lighthearted that brings you joy, symbolizing the freedom of traveling lighter. Notice how your perspective feels different when you are not weighed down.
Saturday.prayer: Thank You, Jesus, for the freedom that comes from checking my baggage with You. Renew my strength and fill me with joy as I hope in You.
